title: Waiting Between Meat and Dairy: The Different Customs Explained
title_zh: 肉类和奶制品之间的等待时间:不同习俗详解
title_he: המתנה בין בשר לחלב: המנהגים השונים מוסברים
description: A comprehensive guide to the different Jewish customs for waiting between eating meat and dairy — from one hour to six hours — exploring the Talmudic sources, cultural wisdom, and practical implementation for families.

chinese_dynasty_references.dynasty: Wei-Jin-Northern and Southern Dynasties (魏晋南北朝)
chinese_dynasty_references.jewish_context: Talmudic Period (~200-500 CE) when basic principles were established

chinese_dynasty_references.dynasty: Song, Yuan, and Ming Dynasties (宋元明)
chinese_dynasty_references.jewish_context: Rishonim Period (~1000-1500 CE) when major codifiers like Rambam established customs

cultural_parallels: Confucian li (礼) and mindful eating practices
Chinese tea ceremony (茶道 chádào) timing vs. waiting between meat and dairy
Buddhist mindful eating vs. Jewish intentional consumption
Traditional Chinese patience and discipline values
